Status (P/F/B): Enumerators) are loaded and displayed correctly
column6:
1. User Management page loads successfully
2. Three tabs display: "Admins", "Supervisors", and "Enumerators"
3. All users are loaded and displayed in respective tables
4. Loading indicators show during data fetch
5. Statistics cards display correct counts for each role
6. Error messages display if loading fails

Objective: Verify that admin can view complete user profile information for any user
Expected Results:
1. Clicking "View Profile" button opens profile view dialog
2. Dialog header displays: "View [Role] Profile"
3. Profile displays user basic information: Name, CID, Email, Phone, Role, Created Date
4. For Supervisors: displays assigned dzongkhags
5. For Enumerators: displays survey assignments (active and ended)
6. Profile data loads correctly from API
7. Error message displays if profile fails to load

Objective: Verify that admin can create a new admin user successfully
Expected Results:
1. "Add Admin" button opens "Create New Admin" dialog
2. Dialog displays form fields: Full Name, CID, Email Address, Phone Number, Password, Confirm Password, Role
3. Form validation works: required fields, email format, CID (11 digits), phone (8 digits), password (min 6 chars), password match
4. Submitting valid form creates admin user
5. Success message displays: "Admin created successfully"
6. Dialog closes and Admins table refreshes with new user
7. Error messages display for validation failures or server errors

Objective: Verify that admin can update admin user information
Expected Results:
1. Clicking "Edit" button on admin row opens "Edit Admin" dialog
2. Dialog pre-fills with current user data: Name, CID (disabled), Email, Phone, Role
3. CID field is disabled and cannot be edited
4. Form validation works for editable fields
5. Submitting with changes updates user successfully
6. Success message displays: "Admin updated successfully"
7. Dialog closes and table refreshes with updated data
8. If no changes made, info message: "No changes to save"
9. Error messages display for validation failures or server errors

Objective: Verify that admin can view admin user profile details

Objective: Verify that admin can assign dzongkhags to a supervisor
Expected Results:
1. Clicking "Assign Dzongkhags" button on supervisor row opens "Manage Dzongkhag Assignments" dialog
2. Dialog displays all available dzongkhags in a multi-select interface
3. Currently assigned dzongkhags are pre-selected
4. Admin can select/deselect dzongkhags
5. Clicking "Save" updates assignments
6. Success message displays: "Dzongkhag assignments updated successfully"
7. Dialog closes and Supervisors table refreshes
8. Assigned dzongkhags are displayed in supervisor row
9. If no changes made, info message: "No changes to save"
10. Error messages display if save fails

Objective: Verify that admin can reset any user's password
Expected Results:
1. Clicking "Reset Password" button opens "Reset Password" dialog
2. Dialog displays user name and form fields: New Password, Confirm Password
3. Password validation: minimum 8 characters, must contain at least 3 of: uppercase, lowercase, number, special character
4. Password strength indicator shows weak/medium/strong
5. Password match validation works
6. Submitting valid password resets user password successfully
7. Success message displays: "Password reset successfully"
8. Dialog closes
9. User can login with new password
10. Error messages display for validation failures or server errors
